steps.text: Remove the parentheses (no sign change needed since both groups are added): $$4nr^{2}+6nr+7n^{2}r+2nr$$
Identify like terms: \(6nr\) and \(2nr\) both have the factor \(nr\). The terms \(4nr^{2}\) and \(7n^{2}r\) have no matching terms.

• A — \(11n^{2}r^{2}+8nr\) incorrectly multiplies \(4nr^{2}\) and \(7n^{2}r\) instead of keeping them separate. Eliminated.
  • B — \(11n^{3}r^{3}+8n^{2}r^{2}\) multiplies exponents, which is not what addition does. Eliminated.
  • C — \(28n^{3}r^{3}+12n^{2}r^{2}\) multiplies the two groups together entirely. Eliminated.
  • D — \(4nr^{2}+7n^{2}r+8nr\) matches the simplified result. That matches choice D — D is correct.
